fact: Japanese uses three distinct writing systems simultaneously: Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji.
samplePhrase: Konnichiwa, o-genki desu ka? Kyou wa ii tenki desu ne.

fact: French was the official language of the English court for nearly 300 years after the Norman Conquest.
samplePhrase: Bonjour, comment allez-vous aujourd'hui ? Il fait un temps magnifique.

fact: German is famous for creating ultra-long compound words, such as 'Rindfleischetikettierungsüberwachungsaufgabenübertragungsgesetz'.
samplePhrase: Guten Tag, wie geht es Ihnen heute? Das Wetter ist wirklich wunderschön.

fact: Spanish is the world's second-most spoken native language, with over 480 million native speakers across 21 countries.
samplePhrase: Hola, ¿cómo estás hoy? Hace un día estupendo para pasear.

fact: Modern standard Italian is primarily derived from the Florentine dialect used by Dante Alighieri in the 14th century.
samplePhrase: Ciao, come stai oggi? È una bellissima giornata per andare al mare.

fact: Only about 5% of the world's Portuguese speakers actually live in Portugal; the vast majority live in Brazil.
samplePhrase: Olá, como vai você hoje? O clima está ótimo para um café.

fact: Russian is one of the six official languages of the United Nations and the most widely spoken Slavic language.
samplePhrase: Zdravstvuyte, kak vashi dela? Segodnya prekrasnaya pogoda.

fact: Mandarin is a tonal language with four main tones; changing the tone of a syllable completely changes its meaning.
samplePhrase: Nǐ hǎo, jīntiān zěnme yàng? Jīntiān de tiānqì zhēn hǎo.

fact: Despite being a Germanic language, roughly 60% of English vocabulary comes from Latin and French origins.
samplePhrase: Hello there, how are you doing today? It is a wonderful day to learn something new.

fact: Arabic is written from right to left, and its cursive script means almost all letters connect within a word.
samplePhrase: Marhaban, kayfa haluka al-yawm? Al-jawwu jamilun jiddan.

fact: Hindi is written in the Devanagari script, which hangs from a distinctive horizontal line called the 'shirorekha'.
samplePhrase: Namaste, aap kaise hain? Aaj ka mausam bohot accha hai.

fact: The Korean alphabet, Hangul, was invented in 1443 by King Sejong the Great specifically to increase literacy among commoners.
samplePhrase: Annyeonghaseyo, o-neul gi-bun-i eo-tteo-sin-ga-yo? Nal-ssi-ga cham jo-seum-ni-da.

fact: Turkish features vowel harmony and extensive agglutination, allowing entire sentences to be expressed in a single word.
samplePhrase: Merhaba, bugün nasılsınız? Hava gerçekten çok güzel.

fact: Dutch is considered the closest major language to English, sharing many grammatical similarities and vocabulary roots.
samplePhrase: Hallo, hoe gaat het vandaag met jou? Het is prachtig weer buiten.

fact: Swedish has a tonal pitch accent that can change a word's meaning depending on whether tone rises or falls on syllables.
samplePhrase: Hej, hur mår du idag? Det är ett fantastiskt väder ute.

fact: Polish has a complex system of seven grammatical cases and is renowned for tongue-twisting consonant clusters.
samplePhrase: Dzień dobry, jak się dzisiaj masz? Pogoda jest wspaniała.

fact: Greek has the longest documented history of any living Indo-European language, spanning over 3,400 years of written records.
samplePhrase: Yia sas, ti kanete simera? O kairos einai yperochos.

fact: Vietnamese is a tonal language with six distinct phonemic tones and uses a Latin-based alphabet introduced in the 17th century.
samplePhrase: Xin chào, hôm nay bạn thế nào? Thời tiết hôm nay thật tuyệt vời.

fact: The Thai alphabet has 44 consonant symbols and 15 vowel symbols, written with no spaces between words in a sentence.
samplePhrase: Sawatdee khrap, wanni pen yangrai bang? Aakat dee mak khrap.

fact: Indonesian is a standardized register of Malay and has no verb tenses, grammatical gender, or plural word modifications.
samplePhrase: Halo, apa kabar hari ini? Cuaca sangat cerah dan menyenangkan.

fact: Finnish is not related to most European languages; it belongs to the Uralic family and has 15 grammatical noun cases.
samplePhrase: Hei, mitä kuuluu tänään? Sää on todella kaunis ja aurinkoinen.

fact: Hungarian features 18 grammatical noun cases and agglutination, making it one of Europe's most unique and challenging languages.
samplePhrase: Szia, hogy vagy ma? Nagyon szép időnk van most.

fact: Czech features words made entirely of consonants without any vowels, such as 'Strč prst skrz krk' (put your finger through your throat).
samplePhrase: Ahoj, jak se dnes máš? Dnes je venku opravdu krásné počasí.

fact: Romanian is the only Eastern Romance language that retained three genders from Latin and preserved the Latin grammatical declension system.
samplePhrase: Salut, ce mai faci astăzi? Vremea este minunată pentru o plimbare.

fact: Ukrainian was voted one of the most melodic languages in the world at a linguistic contest in Paris in 1934.
samplePhrase: Pryvit, yak tvoyi spravy? Syohodni chudova Sonyachna pohoda.

fact: Swahili serves as a lingua franca across much of East Africa and has absorbed many loanwords from Arabic and Portuguese.
samplePhrase: Habari gani leo? Hali ya hewa ni nzuri sana leo.

fact: Tagalog (and Filipino) uses a unique voice-focus system where the verb form signals whether the subject is the actor or the object.
samplePhrase: Kamusta ka ngayon? Napakaganda ng panahon sa araw na ito.

fact: Hebrew is the only ancient dead language in human history that was successfully revived as a modern native tongue.
samplePhrase: Shalom, ma shlomkha ha-yom? Mezeg ha-avir pashut nifla.

fact: Danish features a vocal feature called 'stød', a type of laryngeal catch or creaky voice that distinguishes otherwise identical words.
samplePhrase: Hej, hvordan har du det i dag? Det er et dejligt vejr udenfor.

fact: Norway has two official written standards: Bokmål (derived from Danish) and Nynorsk (based on Norwegian dialects).
samplePhrase: Hei, hvordan går det med deg i dag? Været er helt fantastisk.

fact: International Mother Language Day was established by UNESCO to commemorate Bengali language movement martyrs in Dhaka in 1952.
samplePhrase: Nomoshkar, apni kemon achen? Ajker abohawa khub-i bhalo.

fact: Persian (Farsi) has changed so little over the last millennium that modern speakers can easily read poems by Rumi or Ferdowsi from the 11th century.
samplePhrase: Salam, hal-e shoma chetor ast? Emrouz hava besyar aali ast.

fact: Catalan is not a dialect of Spanish; it evolved independently from Vulgar Latin and shares closer linguistic ties to Occitan in southern France.
samplePhrase: Hola, com estàs avui? Fa un dia meravellós per passejar.

fact: Bulgarian is unique among Slavic languages for having lost almost all grammatical noun cases and developed a suffixed definite article.
samplePhrase: Zdraveyte, kak ste dnes? Vremeto e chudesno za razhodka.

fact: Icelandic has preserved ancient Old Norse grammar and vocabulary so well that modern Icelanders can read 1,000-year-old Viking sagas with ease.
samplePhrase: Halló, hvernig hefurðu það í dag? Veðrið er alveg frábært.
